summary: WebXR failing to start on PC with `Unable to make WebGL context compatible with WebXR`

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible since Godot 4.4-ish

### System information

Windows 11 - Godot 4.5.beta1 - Compatibility Renderer

### Issue description

I'm splitting this issue out from https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/107739 on behalf of the original reporter (@RumarioVR) because it is unrelated to the main problem there (which is now fixed).

> By the way, WebXR hasn't worked with OpenXR and PCVR for quite some time, at least since 4.5 or 4.4. At least for me, with Windows 11 and the latest Chrome browser. That's worth checking out, too.

...

> I'm using Quest with Virtual Desktop and VDXR. I'm using Chrome version 138.0.7204.96 (Official Build) (64-bit). I don't have any special or experimental WebXR/WebGPU flags set in Chrome, just the default settings. I've tested other WebXR frameworks like Playcanvas or BabylonJS and some games without issues. For testing, I used the latest Godot XR Tools demo. It looks fine at first. Then I press the ENTER VR button. It looks like it's switching, but then I get this error message: Context lost.
> 
> ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/38d0f38a-3a75-4c12-913d-36e714138ed9)

summary: VisualShaderNodeCustom Vector4D output causes error when expanding/decomposing it through the Visual Shader editor.
Line2D Rendering Inconsistency Between Vulkan and OpenGL Backends

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Windows11 v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6] Vulkan 1.4.303 - Forward+ - Using Device #0: NVIDIA - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Laptop GPU

### Issue description


When using Line2D to create dynamic trails (continuously adding/removing points each frame), the trail renders correctly when:
• Using the OpenGL backend

• When pausing the game

• When resizing the game window

However, the trail fails to render during normal gameplay when using the Vulkan backend, despite:
• Point data being correctly updated

• queue_redraw() being called

• No errors in the debug console


### Steps to reproduce


1. Create a new scene with a moving RigidBody2D (e.g., a ball with physics)
2. Add a Line2D node as a child
3. Implement trail logic:
   extends Line2D
   
   @export var trail_length = 50
   
   func _process(delta):
       var new_point = get_parent().global_position
       add_point(new_point)
       while get_point_count() > trail_length:
           remove_point(0)
       queue_redraw()
   
4. Run project with Vulkan backend (default)
5. Observe: Trail doesn't render during movement
6. Pause game: Trail appears correctly
7. Switch to OpenGL backend (rendering/renderer/rendering_method = "gl_compatibility")
8. Run project: Trail renders correctly during movement

Expected Behavior

Line2D should render dynamically updated points consistently across all rendering backends, without requiring special handling or workarounds.

Actual Behavior

• OpenGL backend: Works as expected

• Vulkan backend: 

  • Points are added/removed correctly (verified via print(get_point_count()))

  • No rendering occurs during normal gameplay

  • Rendering appears immediately upon pausing or window resize


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[Flyball.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21458318/Flyball.zip)

summary: When using a grouped animation state machine in an inner blend tree in an outer state machine: _get_parent_playback: "Can't get parent AnimationNodeStateMachinePlayback with path: parameters/root_state/aiming/playback. Maybe there is no Root/Nested AnimationNodeStateMachine in the parent of the Grouped AnimationNodeStateMachine."

description.text: ### Tested versions 4.4.1.stable ### System information Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-13700K (24 threads) ### Issue description I have an animation tree, with a top level blend tree, a state machine in that blend tree, and a grouped state machine in a blend tree inside of the first state machine. (so blend tree -> state machine in tree -> blend tree as state -> state machine in tree). When I try to use the root playback to travel the grouped state machine, I get the following error: ```E 0:00:06:981 _get_parent_playback: Can't get parent AnimationNodeStateMachinePlayback with path: parameters/root_state/aiming/playback. Maybe there is no Root/Nested AnimationNodeStateMachine in the parent of the Grouped AnimationNodeStateMachine. scene/animation/animation_node_state_machine.cpp:1152 @ _get_parent_playback()``` I suspect this is because the inner state machine exists in an inner blend tree, but the documentation for grouped implies this is okay: > This is a grouped state machine that can be controlled from a parent state machine. It does not work independently. There must be a state machine with state_machine_type of STATE_MACHINE_TYPE_ROOT or STATE_MACHINE_TYPE_NESTED in the parent or ancestor. It's possible I'm misunderstanding this documentation and the ancestors all need to be state machines, not a blend tree between state machines. But as written there is an appropriate state machine in the ancestor ### Steps to reproduce It's possible I'm calling the nested state machine wrong, if I make it not a grouped machine I can correctly access the two playbacks via `parameters/root_state/playback` and `parameters/root_state/aiming/aim_state/playback` When grouped I've tried several different strings to no avail. I've tried calling `root.travel("aiming")` -- also `"aiming/aim_state"` & `"aiming/aim_state/RESET"` -- which all generate the error: in addition to several others like `aiming/RESET` which produce useful errors indicating the path is wrong which eventually lead me to believe the paths above are correct and something might be wrong either with my understanding of the documentation or a bug. In the editor, this is the path to the inner state machine: with the inner state machine looking like: and with the inner blend tree looking like: and the outer state machine looking like (this was a bit simpler without pre- and post- nodes until i tried to fix it with the information in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/75759): and finally the outer blend tree just having the state root state machine in it in addition to some oneshots and other branches. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) I can try to create an MRP later, but creating these trees is a bit time consuming for me and I suspect I might just be doing something not intended, so I want to make sure that's not the case before I invest in this.

summary: Deselecting Path3D without Curve3D assigned crashes the editor

description.text:

Reproducible in: v4.5.beta3
Not reproducible in: v4.4.1.stable

Godot v4.5.beta3 - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1660 SUPER (NVIDIA; 31.0.15.3713) - AMD Ryzen 5 3600 6-Core Processor (12 threads) - 31.92 GiB memory

### Issue description

When adding a Path3D to the scene, a new Curve3D is automatically created and assigned to it. If you revert the `curve` property of the Path3D in the Inspector to `null`, deselecting the Path3D node in any way (selecting another node, deleting the Path3D node, etc.) crashes the editor.

In v4.4.1.stable, doing the same thing will deselect the Path3D node without a crash.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fae75349-1685-4968-80b5-b94da5f61b86




### Steps to reproduce

1. Add a Path3D node
2. Revert the `curve` property
3. Click anywhere to deselect the Path3D node
4. Crash

summary: Moving mouse around causes FPS drops, for some reason

description.text:
### Tested versions

-Reproducible in 4.4.1 stable

### System information

Windows 11 24H2 - 4.4.1 stable

### Issue description

Simply moving the mouse around causes the FPS to drop.

The attached video and project files provided show a very simple scene which, even after dropping almost 1000+ FPS, is still high in the 3000 fps range, **but this issue is also affecting a project of mine**, which can drop the FPS **_from 1000 to around ~500-ish_**, in a 2D game, on a mid-range computer with a 4060 and a Ryzen 5.

Hoping the performance drop isn't as bad on lower end systems.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/22ed7cc8-9773-41fd-9fa0-b0df465e093f

I've heard from another possible related issue that this has to do with a high polling rate? 
Is there anything I can do to fix this?

Is this even something that can reasonably affect other players?

Thanks!


### Steps to reproduce

-create project

-disable vsync

-move mouse around

-fps drops

[mouse_fps.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21455713/mouse_fps.zip)

description.text: ### Tested versions - Tested: v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6] ### System information Godot v4.4.1.stable - Manjaro Linux #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu, 10 Jul 2025 15:37:43 +0000 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Mesa Intel(R) UHD Graphics 620 (KBL GT2) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-8350U CPU @ 1.70GHz (8 threads) ### Issue description When attaching a RigidBody3D to a Generic6DOFJoint3D, the joint unexpectedly rotates when enabling `axis_lock_angular_x` or `axis_lock_angular_y` on the RigidBody3D. summary: InputMap.get_actions() only lists default actions when called in tool mode; custom actions appear only in-game

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in:3.6 Stable,3.6.1 Stable,4.4.1 Stable

### System information

Xubuntu 20.04 Godot 3.6.1.stable - GLES3 Intel i5 generation 2 integrated gpu 

### Issue description

In Godot 3.x, when running a script in tool mode, InputMap.get_actions() only returns the default input actions. Custom actions defined in the project settings are not included unless the project is running (in-game).

### Steps to reproduce
1- Create a new Godot 3.x or 4.x project.

2- Add at least one custom input action via Project > Project Settings > Input Map (e.g., add "custom_action").

3- Create a new script with tool mode enabled and attach it to a Node in the scene.

4- In the _ready() function, print the list of input actions using print(InputMap.get_actions()).

5- Observe the output in the editor console.

Expected Behavior
InputMap.get_actions() should return all actions, including custom ones defined in the project settings, even when running in tool mode (editor).

Actual Behavior
Only the default input actions are returned in tool mode. Custom actions are missing. However, when the scene is run (in-game), all actions are returned correctly.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[Issue Input.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21446187/Issue.Input.zip)
